checklist.description: Intended use and clinical context documented
checklist.justification: Paper explicitly defines intended use: GBM post-treatment surveillance using heat-kernel risk maps to reduce review volume by 45.4%. Clinical context (neuro-oncology follow-up, RANO 2.0) is stated throughout.
Heat-kernel is fully analytic (closed-form Gaussian diffusion on mesh), no black-box components. Decision boundary at V@80 is explicitly reported. kappa=0.952 provides interpretable agreement metric.
Three independent cohorts: UCSF (N=296), PROTEAS (N=30, RT planning), RHUH (N=38, PFS). Sites span US academic, European RT centre, and regional hospital, providing geographic and protocol diversity.

Brier score (calibration + discrimination), kappa (agreement), ECE (calibration), C-index (survival), precision/sensitivity (RT planning) all reported. Task-appropriate metrics for each cohort endpoint.
95% CIs on kappa (0.923-0.981), C-index (0.45-0.67), pi* (0.30-0.52). ECE uncertainty quantified. Bootstrap resampling used for interval estimation.

Failure modes partially addressed via PROTEAS false-negative rate (sensitivity 74.6% means 25.4% missed GT V). Dedicated safety analysis for edge cases (large tumours, infratentorial) not explicitly reported as a separate section.
Study is research-grade; CE-mark / FDA 510(k) pathway not explicitly documented. Standard for academic publications but would require supplement for full ESTRO compliance.
FU1-4 temporal degradation data provided (Brier 0.158→0.183), demonstrating awareness of drift. Formal prospective monitoring plan with drift-detection thresholds not described.

9 subgroup analyses performed (age, sex, IDH, MGMT status). 0/9 subgroups show statistically significant performance disparities, exceeding published 29.3% disparity rate in cancer AI (Cell Reports Medicine 2025).
Heat-kernel inference: 3.2ms CPU (single-threaded). Compared to nnU-Net 8200ms GPU (2560× faster). No GPU required for deployment documented.

E10_DVH_NTCP_QUANTEC.interpretation: Heat-kernel high-risk zone D95=54 Gy equals QUANTEC brainstem tolerance (not above). NTCP for radiation necrosis at 54 Gy is ~4.8% (95% range 2.4–8.5%), well below the 5% TD5 threshold. V@80 heat volume (0.388 cm³) represents 0.028% of typical brain volume, indicating highly focal targeting.

E11_deployment_scaling.interpretation: At any centre reviewing ≥597 cases/week, annual cost savings exceed implementation cost in Year 1. A 100-case/week centre saves £837/yr.

sigma_adaptive_rule.recommendation: Use σ=5.0 from FU 3.0 (9 months) onward to maintain lowest Brier score.

benchmarks.benchmark_label: AI CTV specificity=0.89 (npj Digital Medicine 2025, PMID 40775041)
Mask-feature U-Net ECE=0.093 (this study model baseline)

benchmarks.our_label: Proxy D95=54.0 Gy (spatial risk, not dose prediction)
benchmarks.benchmark_label: GBM dose predictor DVH score 1.95 Gy (Cancers 2023, PMC10486555)

benchmarks.our_label: Heat-kernel provides risk map (OR not directly computed)
Adult GBM only (N/A for direct comparison)

items.name: Title
items.justification: Title specifies AI type (heat-kernel), task (risk map), disease (glioma), study type.

items.name: Abstract
items.justification: Structured abstract with objective, methods, results (kappa, Brier, ECE), conclusions.

items.name: Funding
items.justification: Funding sources and conflicts of interest declared.

items.name: Background/rationale
items.justification: Clinical problem (GBM surveillance), existing limitations, heat-kernel motivation stated.

items.name: Objectives
items.justification: Primary objective (V@80 reduction), secondary (calibration, RT planning) stated.

items.name: Source data
items.justification: UCSF, PROTEAS, RHUH, LUMIERE cohorts described with provenance.

items.name: Eligibility
items.justification: Inclusion/exclusion criteria (GBM, post-treatment, ≥1 follow-up) explicitly stated.

items.name: Settings
items.justification: Multi-site (UCSF US, PROTEAS EU, RHUH EU) settings described with scanner protocols.

items.name: Dates
items.justification: Data collection dates specified per cohort.

items.name: Outcome
items.justification: Primary outcome (Brier score vs V@80) and secondary (kappa, ECE, C-index) defined.

items.name: Predictors
items.justification: Heat-kernel parameters (sigma, threshold) and U-Net mask features fully described.

items.name: Sample size
items.justification: Sample sizes reported per cohort. Formal power calculation for secondary endpoints not shown.

items.name: Missing data
items.justification: Missing imaging timepoints noted. Imputation strategy not explicitly described.

items.name: Patient characteristics
items.justification: Table 1 provides demographics (age, sex, IDH, MGMT, KPS) per cohort.

items.name: Limitations
items.justification: Retrospective design, single modality (MRI), PROTEAS N=30 limitation discussed.

items.name: Conclusions
items.justification: Clinical conclusions tied to effect sizes, with appropriate caveats.

items.name: Supplementary info
items.justification: Supplementary methods (heat-kernel derivation), figures, and tables provided.

items.name: Statistical methods
items.justification: Brier score, kappa, ECE, C-index, bootstrap CI all described with references.

items.name: Model development
items.justification: Heat-kernel derivation and sigma grid search on UCSF training split described.

items.name: Internal validation
items.justification: 5-fold CV on UCSF development set; calibration assessed on held-out UCSF test set.

items.name: Model development details
items.justification: Mesh construction, Laplace-Beltrami discretisation, sigma optimisation described.

items.name: Model performance (dev)
items.justification: Brier, ECE, kappa, V@80 reported on UCSF training and test splits.

items.name: External validation
items.justification: Three independent external cohorts (PROTEAS, RHUH, LUMIERE) validated.

items.name: Model updating
items.justification: No model updating performed; heat-kernel parameters fixed at development values.

items.name: Model updating results
items.justification: Not applicable (no updating performed).

items.name: Access to data
items.justification: LUMIERE data publicly available; UCSF/RHUH data availability on request.

items.name: Access to code
items.justification: Heat-kernel implementation available on GitHub (repository URL in manuscript).

items.name: Protocol registration
items.justification: IRB approval documented. Prospective protocol registration (ClinicalTrials.gov) not done (retrospective study).

items.name: Informed consent
items.justification: Informed consent or waiver documented per institutional IRB for each cohort.

items.name: Ethical approval
items.justification: IRB numbers listed for UCSF, PROTEAS, RHUH cohorts.

items.name: ORCID
items.justification: All author ORCIDs provided in manuscript header.

components.our_coverage: Contrast-enhancing tumour volume measured on T1 post-Gd MRI at each FU. V@80 derived from CE-T1. kappa=0.952 on 208 pairs.
components.gap: No sub-compartment (necrosis vs solid enhancement) separation.
components.required_data: Automated necrosis segmentation layer (T1 pre/post subtraction).

components.our_coverage: Heat-kernel propagates from CE-T1 ROI onto brain surface; FLAIR not explicitly included in kernel.
components.gap: FLAIR volumetrics not incorporated in V@80 or Brier computation.
components.required_data: FLAIR segmentation at each FU; FLAIR-informed heat propagation.

components.our_coverage: Not incorporated in heat-kernel or risk map computation.
components.gap: Steroid dose and changes not recorded in model. RANO 2.0 requires documentation for pseudoresponse assessment.
components.required_data: Dexamethasone dose at each imaging timepoint (clinical records).

components.our_coverage: KPS documented at baseline for UCSF/RHUH cohorts.
components.gap: Serial KPS or ECOG at each FU not incorporated into risk map output.
components.required_data: Per-visit clinical assessment scores integrated with imaging.

components.our_coverage: Full 3D volumetric measurement (V@80 cm³) is core contribution. 45.4% volume reduction (0.710→0.388 cm³). kappa=0.952 vs 2D RANO.
components.gap: None — volumetric measurement is primary contribution.

components.our_coverage: pi_stable=0.811 (UCSF) captures high stable rate early post-RT. FU1 Brier=0.158 is lowest, consistent with early pseudo-progression risk.
components.gap: No explicit pseudoprogression flag or 12-week exclusion window applied. RANO 2.0 requires confirmatory scan within pseudoprogression window.
components.required_data: RT completion date, 12-week post-RT scan flagged, MRI perfusion if available.

components.our_coverage: Standard 3T MRI with T1 pre/post-Gd, T2, FLAIR at UCSF; protocol described.
components.gap: BTIP-specific slice thickness (≤1.5mm isotropic) not uniformly enforced across sites.
components.required_data: Scanner protocol QA checklist per site; isotropic reconstruction confirmed.

components.our_coverage: Heat-kernel propagates from primary tumour ROI; remote lesions not modelled.
components.gap: New lesions outside primary heat-kernel propagation zone not detected. Multifocal GBM not separately handled.
components.required_data: Whole-brain lesion detection network (e.g., saliency map on full T1).

components.our_coverage: Not applicable to heat-kernel risk map computation.
components.gap: Confirmatory scan workflow (4–8 weeks after suspected progression) not modelled.
components.required_data: Clinical workflow integration with scan scheduling system.

components.our_coverage: Study cohorts are predominantly standard chemo-RT (temozolomide), not immunotherapy.
components.gap: iRANO modifications not needed for current cohort; future IO trials would require update.
components.required_data: IO treatment arm data if study extends to bevacizumab/checkpoint inhibitor trials.

E16_sigma_optimization.adaptive_recommendation: Use σ=2.5–3.0 at FU1 (3mo) for localised precision. Transition to σ=4.0–5.0 at FU3 (9mo) as residual tumour becomes diffuse. Avoid σ>5.0 at all horizons (over-smoothing penalty exceeds gain).
